book_claims.claim: White bread has a very high glycemic index (~95) and should be minimized; whole-grain bread is strongly preferred in the Longevity Diet
book_claims.mechanism: Refined white flour is rapidly digested to glucose, causing insulin spikes; whole-grain fiber slows digestion and flattens the glycemic response

book_claims.claim: Whole-wheat bread provides calcium (30 mg per slice, 3% DV), iron (1 mg per slice, 6% DV), and magnesium (46 mg per 2 slices, 12% DV); 40–80 g per meal recommended; dark bread with olive oil was a staple of Molochio centenarian Salvatore Caruso
book_claims.mechanism: Fortified whole-grain breads supply folate, calcium, iron, and magnesium; modest portions with olive oil reduce glycemic load and add anti-inflammatory fats

supplementary_research.finding: PMID 30418616: Whole-grain bread consumption is inversely associated with all-cause mortality in large prospective cohorts; refined-grain bread shows no protective association
PMID 27301975: Whole-grain intake is associated with reduced risk of cardiovascular disease, type 2 diabetes, and colorectal cancer; each 28 g/day increment confers ~9% lower CVD mortality

Whole-grain bread contains beta-glucan, arabinoxylans, and resistant starch that feed gut microbiota; sourdough fermentation further lowers the glycemic index to ~54 compared to ~70 for conventional whole-wheat

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Insoluble and soluble fiber; whole-grain varieties have significantly more than white bread (~2.7 g)
Bioavailability ~40–60%; phytate can limit absorption — sourdough fermentation reduces phytate
Non-heme iron; vitamin C co-ingestion substantially improves absorption
Enriched breads restore B vitamins lost in milling; whole-grain retains naturally occurring B vitamins
Highly bioavailable organic selenomethionine form; important for thyroid function and antioxidant defense

synergies.description: Olive oil's monounsaturated fats slow gastric emptying, further lowering the glycemic load of bread and adding cardiovascular-protective polyphenols
Bread and legumes together provide complementary amino acid profiles (lysine-limited grain + methionine-limited legume) for complete protein
Vitamin C in tomatoes enhances non-heme iron absorption from whole-grain bread
Walnuts add omega-3 ALA and additional fiber; walnut bread is an explicitly named longevity-diet food in The Longevity Diet
